top10.introHtml: Refreshed 2026-05-20. Criteria: $7k–$15k, 35 mi of Media PA 19063, no accidents / clean title where filtered. Ranked for cheap fun second-car ownership — top condition and reliability weight most.

picks.whyGood: The 2.8-liter inline-6 Z3 is one of the best-balanced cheap roadsters money can buy — more power than the 1.9, better brakes, same bulletproof M52 block. Clean title AND no accidents on a private listing at $7,940 with only 75k miles is genuinely rare. Private Seller Exchange means you can inspect it without a dealer upsell. Manual preferred — verify transmission.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate soft top through full cycle, check rear plastic window for cracks/hazing. Inspect rocker panels for rust (Z3 Achilles heel). Timing chain rattle on cold start (M52 stretch). Cooling system — check for white steam or sweet smell. Power steering pump groan. Test all electrics.
picks.bottomLine: Best value performance roadster in the search — clean history, right mileage, right price.

picks.whyGood: CarGurus flags this as a Great Deal at $7,500 for a 2006 Solstice with under 63k miles — that's excellent for this model. The Solstice is a proper roadster on the Kappa platform with good aftermarket support, and 63k miles is the sweet spot where the car is broken in without being worn. 27 miles from Media is an easy drive.
picks.whatToVerify: Inspect the convertible top latch and weatherstripping — Solstice tops are known to leak at the A-pillar corners. Check for rocker panel and frame rail rust (Kappa cars can trap water). Run a CarFax for flood/accident history. Verify the ecotec engine has no oil consumption issues (do a compression test). Ask for service records.
picks.bottomLine: Best pure fun-per-dollar in this search — low miles, Great Deal badge, proven roadster platform.

picks.whyGood: The E46 325Ci convertible is arguably the most beautiful car at this price point — proper 4-seater drop-top with rear seats that actually work, silky M54 inline-6, and only 60,000 miles at $10k is exceptional. No accidents reported. Only 15 miles from Media.
picks.whatToVerify: E46 convertible top: operate through full open/close cycle, listen for hydraulic pump strain, check top frame alignment. Inspect sub-frame mounting points for cracking (known E46 issue — look underneath with a flashlight). Check for rear shock tower rust. Cooling system: MINI-Cooper style plastic thermostat housing — ask if updated. Verify odometer reading with service records.
picks.bottomLine: The most complete convertible in the list — four seats, great engine, beautiful styling, low miles.

picks.whyGood: Only 9 miles from Media, 70k miles, $10,099 at a BMW-specialist shop (Premier Eurocars). The Z4 E85 is a sharp-handling roadster with an excellent 2.5-liter inline-6. This is the closest quality car in the entire search.
picks.whatToVerify: Z4 top: the fabric is often in good shape but check the tension cables and rear window delamination. Inspect the sill panels for rust bubbling. Check for rear differential whine. Verify cooling — E46-era engines share the same plastic coolant components. Ask dealer if they have service records.
picks.bottomLine: Closest to home, BMW specialist dealer, and excellent miles — hard to beat for convenience and value.

picks.whyGood: The GXP is the turbocharged variant of the Solstice — 260hp from the LNF turbo-4, which makes this a legitimate sports car at $8,590. CarGurus calls it a Great Deal. The 2009 is the last model year (freshest possible). 88k miles is fine for a turbo-4 if it's been maintained.
picks.whatToVerify: GXP-specific: check intercooler condition and inspect for boost leaks (cracked couplers). The LNF turbo-4 likes to run hot — look for signs of overheating. The clutch on manuals can be worn by enthusiastic drivers. Same top and rust checks as the base Solstice. Ask for turbo service history.
picks.bottomLine: Turbocharged Solstice at a Great Deal price — more car than most listings at this budget.

picks.whyGood: The R170 SLK 230 with the retractable hardtop is the ultimate cheap-fun convertible concept — elegant, weathertight, and low-maintenance compared to soft-tops. 68k miles at $8,995 with no accidents from a franchise dealer (Kia of West Chester carries trade-ins). The kompressor makes it entertaining even at these miles.
picks.whatToVerify: Retractable hardtop mechanism: operate top fully (takes ~25 seconds) — listen for hydraulic strain and check that trunk latch engages cleanly. Check for electrical gremlins (SLK of this era is prone to window switch failures). Verify serpentine belt and kompressor belt condition. Look for coolant weeps at the thermostat housing.
picks.bottomLine: Retractable hardtop security at a soft-top price — 10 miles away and no accidents reported.

picks.whyGood: The 1 Series convertible is BMW's last proper RWD small convertible before the brand went all-FWD. The N52 inline-6 in the 128i is one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. Under 70k miles and CarGurus rates it a Great Deal at $9,500.
picks.whatToVerify: 1 Series convertible top: check hydraulic rams for leaks and verify full cycle operation. Inspect for rust on the A-pillar drain channels. N52 engine — verify VANOS operation (idle rough at cold start = worn solenoids). Check for oil leaks at valve cover gasket. Ask for service records confirming oil change intervals.
picks.bottomLine: BMW's last great small RWD convertible — Great Deal badge at a very fair price with low miles.

picks.whyGood: A 2001 Boxster with 74k miles, clean title AND no accidents at $11,250 is competitive. The mid-engine layout makes this the best-handling car in the list by a large margin. The 2001 model year uses the M96 engine — IMS bearing is the key risk item, but at 74k it may already have been serviced.
picks.whatToVerify: CRITICAL: Verify IMS (intermediate shaft) bearing service history — this is make-or-break on any pre-2006 Boxster. If documentation exists, this is a buy; if not, get a PPI from a Porsche specialist. Check for RMS (rear main seal) weeping oil. Inspect top for tears and hydraulic top pump function. Verify coolant reservoir for cloudy fluid (head gasket indicator). Budget $500-700 for PPI from a marque specialist.
picks.bottomLine: Best driving experience in the list — just verify the IMS bearing before committing.

picks.whyGood: 30,483 miles on a 2007 Sky Roadster is exceptional — this car has barely been driven. The Kappa platform is the same as the Solstice but in Saturn trim. At $12,995 CarGurus calls it a Fair Deal, meaning there's likely room to negotiate. The near-new mileage means seals, top, and mechanicals are all in far better shape than any high-mileage alternative.
picks.whatToVerify: Same Kappa checks as the Solstice: top seals, A-pillar leaks, rocker rust. With only 30k miles the engine should be excellent — but verify it's been driven regularly (sat cars develop seal issues). Check for interior moisture from the top seal. Negotiate from the Fair Deal price — comparable Solstice roadsters are $4k cheaper.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest mileage convertible in the entire search — at the right price, a fantastic sleeper pick.

picks.whyGood: A 2006 Audi S4 Cabriolet for $7,404 with a Great Deal badge, clean title AND no accidents (Cars.com filtered) is an outrageous value for a 340hp V8 quattro AWD convertible. This is aspirational — it's a $30k+ car new. The B7 S4's 4.2 V8 is a maintenance-intensive engine, but if the service has been done, nothing in this price bracket offers more performance.
picks.whatToVerify: Audi 4.2 V8: the timing chain guides and tensioners are the main failure mode — verify they've been replaced (known recall area). Check for oil consumption (normal at high miles, but excessive means worn rings). Verify all quattro system functions (engage all-wheel drive properly). Soft top: inspect all four corners of the rear window seam — fabric is expensive to replace. Budget for high running costs.
picks.bottomLine: High-risk, high-reward — a supercar-engined convertible at econobox money if the maintenance is current.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles within the search radius and budget. Ranked by trim (GT > base) then mileage.

picks.whyGood: Ford Mustang convertible within budget — verify top condition and rear axle clunk on acceleration.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95/S197 specific: frame rust at convertible top mount to rocker junction, T-3650 third-gear synchro (slow-shift to 3rd), 8.8 rear axle clunk on takeoff, water staining on rear seats from top seal.
picks.bottomLine: Classic American muscle convertible — PPI the top mechanism and axle before committing.

picks.whyGood: Ford Mustang convertible within budget — verify top condition and rear axle clunk on acceleration.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95/S197 specific: frame rust at convertible top mount to rocker junction, T-3650 third-gear synchro (slow-shift to 3rd), 8.8 rear axle clunk on takeoff, water staining on rear seats from top seal.
picks.bottomLine: Classic American muscle convertible — PPI the top mechanism and axle before committing.
